authors_text: Vivian Lai,Alison Smith-Renner,Ke Zhang,Ruijia Cheng,Wenjuan Zhang,Joel Tetreault,Alejandro Jaimes
title: An Exploration of Post-Editing Effectiveness in Text Summarization
title_zh: 文本摘要中的后期编辑有效性探索
abstract: Automatic summarization methods are efficient but can suffer from low quality. In comparison, manual summarization is expensive but produces higher quality. Can humans and AI collaborate to improve summarization performance? In similar text generation tasks (e.g., machine translation), human-AI collaboration in the form of "post-editing" AI-generated text reduces human workload and improves the quality of AI output. Therefore, we explored whether post-editing offers advantages in text summarization. Specifically, we conducted an experiment with 72 participants, comparing post-editing provided summaries with manual summarization for summary quality, human efficiency, and user experience on formal (XSum news) and informal (Reddit posts) text. This study sheds valuable insights on when post-editing is useful for text summarization: it helped in some cases (e.g., when participants lacked domain knowledge) but not in others (e.g., when provided summaries include inaccurate information). Participants' different editing strategies and needs for assistance offer implications for future human-AI summarization systems.
summary: Automatic summarization methods are efficient but can suffer from low quality. In comparison, manual summarization is expensive but produces higher quality. Can humans and AI collaborate to improve summarization performance? In similar text generation tasks (e.g., machine translation), human-AI collaboration in the form of "post-editing" AI-generated text reduces human workload and improves the quality of AI output. Therefore, we explored whether post-editing offers advantages in text summarization. Specifically, we conducted an experiment with 72 participants, comparing post-editing provided summaries with manual summarization for summary quality, human efficiency, and user experience on formal (XSum news) and informal (Reddit posts) text. This study sheds valuable insights on when post-editing is useful for text summarization: it helped in some cases (e.g., when participants lacked domain knowledge) but not in others (e.g., when provided summaries include inaccurate information). Participants' different editing strategies and needs for assistance offer implications for future human-AI summarization systems.
abstract_zh: 自动摘要方法很有效，但质量较低。相比之下，手动摘要成本较高，但质量较高。人类和人工智能可以合作提高摘要性能吗？在类似的文本生成任务（例如机器翻译）中，以“后期编辑”人工智能生成文本的形式进行人类与人工智能协作，可以减少人类工作量并提高人工智能输出的质量。因此，我们探讨了译后编辑在文本摘要方面是否具有优势。具体来说，我们对 72 名参与者进行了一项实验，比较了后期编辑提供的摘要与手动摘要的摘要质量、人员效率以及正式（XSum 新闻）和非正式（Reddit 帖子）文本的用户体验。这项研究为后期编辑何时对文本摘要有用提供了宝贵的见解：它在某些情况下（例如，当参与者缺乏领域知识时）有帮助，但在其他情况下则没有帮助（例如，当提供的摘要包含不准确的信息时）。参与者不同的编辑策略和对帮助的需求为未来的人类人工智能摘要系统提供了启示。

AI.tldr: 探讨后编辑在文本摘要中的有效性，比较其与纯人工摘要的效果
AI.motivation: 自动摘要效率高但质量低，人工成本高，探索人机协作是否能提升效果，参考机器翻译中的后编辑方法
AI.method: 进行72人实验，比较后编辑与人工摘要的质量、效率和用户体验，针对正式（XSum新闻）和非正式（Reddit帖子）文本
AI.result: 后编辑在部分场景有效（如缺乏领域知识），但摘要包含错误信息时无效，参与者有不同的编辑策略和需求
AI.conclusion: 后编辑在文本摘要中特定场景下有效，需根据摘要质量与用户需求优化人机协作系统

Participants' different editing strategies and needs for assistance offer implications for future human-AI summarization systems. 自动摘要方法很有效，但质量较低。相比之下，手动摘要成本较高，但质量较高。人类和人工智能可以合作提高摘要性能吗？在类似的文本生成任务（例如机器翻译）中，以“后期编辑”人工智能生成文本的形式进行人类与人工智能协作，可以减少人类工作量并提高人工智能输出的质量。因此，我们探讨了译后编辑在文本摘要方面是否具有优势。具体来说，我们对 72 名参与者进行了一项实验，比较了后期编辑提供的摘要与手动摘要的摘要质量、人员效率以及正式（XSum 新闻）和非正式（Reddit 帖子）文本的用户体验。这项研究为后期编辑何时对文本摘要有用提供了宝贵的见解：它在某些情况下（例如，当参与者缺乏领域知识时）有帮助，但在其他情况下则没有帮助（例如，当提供的摘要包含不准确的信息时）。参与者不同的编辑策略和对帮助的需求为未来的人类人工智能摘要系统提供了启示。 探讨后编辑在文本摘要中的有效性，比较其与纯人工摘要的效果 Vivian Lai,Alison Smith-Renner,Ke Zhang,Ruijia Cheng,Wenjuan Zhang,Joel Tetreault,Alejandro Jaimes cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.HC

authors_text: Yiran Li,Ze Wang
title: Acceleration of cerebral blood flow and arterial transit time maps estimation from multiple post-labeling delay arterial spin-labeled MRI via deep learning
title_zh: 通过深度学习从多个标记后延迟动脉自旋标记 MRI 估计脑血流加速和动脉传输时间图
abstract: Purpose: Arterial spin labeling (ASL) perfusion imaging indicates direct and absolute measurement of cerebral blood flow (CBF). Arterial transit time (ATT) is a related physiological parameter reflecting the duration for the labeled spins to reach the brain region of interest. Multiple post-labeling delay (PLDs) can provide robust measures of both CBF and ATT, allowing for optimization of regional CBF modeling based on ATT. The prolonged acquisition time can potentially reduce the quality and accuracy of the CBF and ATT estimation. We proposed a novel network to significantly reduce the number of PLDs with higher signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). Method: CBF and ATT estimations were performed for one PLD and two PLDs sepa-rately. Each model was trained independently to learn the nonlinear transformation from perfusion weighted image (PWI) to CBF and ATT images. Results: Both one-PLD and two-PLD models outperformed the conventional method visually on CBF and two-PLD model showed more accurate structure on ATT estima-tion. The proposed method significantly reduces the number of PLDs from 6 to 2 on ATT and even to single PLD on CBF without sacrificing the SNR. Conclusion: It is feasible to generate CBF and ATT maps with reduced PLDs using deep learning with high quality.
summary: Purpose: Arterial spin labeling (ASL) perfusion imaging indicates direct and absolute measurement of cerebral blood flow (CBF). Arterial transit time (ATT) is a related physiological parameter reflecting the duration for the labeled spins to reach the brain region of interest. Multiple post-labeling delay (PLDs) can provide robust measures of both CBF and ATT, allowing for optimization of regional CBF modeling based on ATT. The prolonged acquisition time can potentially reduce the quality and accuracy of the CBF and ATT estimation. We proposed a novel network to significantly reduce the number of PLDs with higher signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). Method: CBF and ATT estimations were performed for one PLD and two PLDs sepa-rately. Each model was trained independently to learn the nonlinear transformation from perfusion weighted image (PWI) to CBF and ATT images. Results: Both one-PLD and two-PLD models outperformed the conventional method visually on CBF and two-PLD model showed more accurate structure on ATT estima-tion. The proposed method significantly reduces the number of PLDs from 6 to 2 on ATT and even to single PLD on CBF without sacrificing the SNR. Conclusion: It is feasible to generate CBF and ATT maps with reduced PLDs using deep learning with high quality.
abstract_zh: 目的：动脉自旋标记 (ASL) 灌注成像可直接、绝对地测量脑血流量 (CBF)。动脉传输时间（ATT）是一个相关的生理参数，反映标记的自旋到达感兴趣的大脑区域的持续时间。多个标记后延迟 (PLD) 可以提供 CBF 和 ATT 的稳健测量，从而可以优化基于 ATT 的区域 CBF 建模。延长的采集时间可能会降低 CBF 和 ATT 估计的质量和准确性。我们提出了一种新颖的网络，可以显着减少具有更高信噪比 (SNR) 的 PLD 数量。方法：分别对一个PLD和两个PLD进行CBF和ATT估计。每个模型都经过独立训练，以学习从灌注加权图像 (PWI) 到 CBF 和 ATT 图像的非线性变换。结果：1-PLD 和 2-PLD 模型在 CBF 上均优于传统方法，并且 2-PLD 模型在 ATT 估计上显示出更准确的结构。所提出的方法将 ATT 上的 PLD 数量从 6 个显着减少到 2 个，甚至在 CBF 上减少到单个 PLD，而不会牺牲 SNR。结论：使用深度学习高质量生成具有减少 PLD 的 CBF 和 ATT 图是可行的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度学习的方法，通过减少多后标记延迟（PLDs）的数量来加速脑血流（CBF）和动脉传递时间（ATT）图的估计。
AI.motivation: 传统多PLD动脉自旋标记（ASL）MRI方法因采集时间长导致信号噪声比（SNR）下降，影响CBF和ATT估计质量。需通过减少PLD数量提升效率。
AI.method: 分别使用单PLD和双PLD独立训练模型，学习从灌注加权图像（PWI）到CBF/ATT图像的非线性变换，通过深度学习实现参数估计。
AI.result: 单PLD和双PLD模型在CBF估计上优于传统方法，双PLD模型在ATT结构准确性上表现更优，PLD数量从6个减少至2个（ATT）甚至1个（CBF）且保持SNR。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习可有效减少PLD数量，生成高质量CBF和ATT图，为ASL灌注成像提供高效解决方案。

authors_text: Christian Nauck,Michael Lindner,Konstantin Schürholt,Frank Hellmann
title: Toward Dynamic Stability Assessment of Power Grid Topologies using Graph Neural Networks
title_zh: 使用图神经网络进行电网拓扑动态稳定性评估
abstract: To mitigate climate change, the share of renewable energies in power production needs to be increased. Renewables introduce new challenges to power grids regarding the dynamic stability due to decentralization, reduced inertia, and volatility in production. Since dynamic stability simulations are intractable and exceedingly expensive for large grids, graph neural networks (GNNs) are a promising method to reduce the computational effort of analyzing the dynamic stability of power grids. As a testbed for GNN models, we generate new, large datasets of dynamic stability of synthetic power grids, and provide them as an open-source resource to the research community. We find that GNNs are surprisingly effective at predicting the highly non-linear targets from topological information only. For the first time, performance that is suitable for practical use cases is achieved. Furthermore, we demonstrate the ability of these models to accurately identify particular vulnerable nodes in power grids, so-called troublemakers. Last, we find that GNNs trained on small grids generate accurate predictions on a large synthetic model of the Texan power grid, which illustrates the potential for real-world applications.
summary: To mitigate climate change, the share of renewable energies in power production needs to be increased. Renewables introduce new challenges to power grids regarding the dynamic stability due to decentralization, reduced inertia, and volatility in production. Since dynamic stability simulations are intractable and exceedingly expensive for large grids, graph neural networks (GNNs) are a promising method to reduce the computational effort of analyzing the dynamic stability of power grids. As a testbed for GNN models, we generate new, large datasets of dynamic stability of synthetic power grids, and provide them as an open-source resource to the research community. We find that GNNs are surprisingly effective at predicting the highly non-linear targets from topological information only. For the first time, performance that is suitable for practical use cases is achieved. Furthermore, we demonstrate the ability of these models to accurately identify particular vulnerable nodes in power grids, so-called troublemakers. Last, we find that GNNs trained on small grids generate accurate predictions on a large synthetic model of the Texan power grid, which illustrates the potential for real-world applications.
abstract_zh: 为了缓解气候变化，需要增加可再生能源在电力生产中的份额。由于分散化、惯性减少和生产波动性，可再生能源给电网的动态稳定性带来了新的挑战。由于动态稳定性模拟对于大型电网而言非常棘手且极其昂贵，因此图神经网络（GNN）是减少分析电网动态稳定性的计算工作量的一种有前景的方法。作为 GNN 模型的测试平台，我们生成了新的大型合成电网动态稳定性数据集，并将它们作为开源资源提供给研究社区。我们发现 GNN 在仅根据拓扑信息预测高度非线性目标方面出人意料地有效。首次实现了适合实际用例的性能。此外，我们还证明了这些模型能够准确识别电网中特定的脆弱节点（即所谓的麻烦制造者）。最后，我们发现在小型网格上训练的 GNN 可以对德克萨斯电网的大型综合模型产生准确的预测，这说明了实际应用的潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出使用图神经网络（GNN）评估电力网络拓扑的动态稳定性，以应对可再生能源带来的挑战。
AI.motivation: 可再生能源的普及导致电力系统动态稳定性问题加剧，传统模拟方法计算成本高且不适用于大规模电网。
AI.method: 生成合成电网动态稳定性数据集，训练GNN模型仅基于拓扑信息预测稳定性，并验证其在大型电网中的泛化能力。
AI.result: GNN模型能有效预测非线性稳定性指标，准确识别脆弱节点，并在德州电网模型中实现实用化性能。
AI.conclusion: GNN为电力系统动态稳定性分析提供高效解决方案，开放数据集推动相关研究，实证其在现实场景中的应用潜力。

authors_text: Wouter Van Gansbeke,Simon Vandenhende,Luc Van Gool
title: Discovering Object Masks with Transformers for Unsupervised Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: 使用 Transformers 发现对象掩码以进行无监督语义分割
abstract: The task of unsupervised semantic segmentation aims to cluster pixels into semantically meaningful groups. Specifically, pixels assigned to the same cluster should share high-level semantic properties like their object or part category. This paper presents MaskDistill: a novel framework for unsupervised semantic segmentation based on three key ideas. First, we advocate a data-driven strategy to generate object masks that serve as a pixel grouping prior for semantic segmentation. This approach omits handcrafted priors, which are often designed for specific scene compositions and limit the applicability of competing frameworks. Second, MaskDistill clusters the object masks to obtain pseudo-ground-truth for training an initial object segmentation model. Third, we leverage this model to filter out low-quality object masks. This strategy mitigates the noise in our pixel grouping prior and results in a clean collection of masks which we use to train a final segmentation model. By combining these components, we can considerably outperform previous works for unsupervised semantic segmentation on PASCAL (+11% mIoU) and COCO (+4% mask AP50). Interestingly, as opposed to existing approaches, our framework does not latch onto low-level image cues and is not limited to object-centric datasets. The code and models will be made available.
summary: The task of unsupervised semantic segmentation aims to cluster pixels into semantically meaningful groups. Specifically, pixels assigned to the same cluster should share high-level semantic properties like their object or part category. This paper presents MaskDistill: a novel framework for unsupervised semantic segmentation based on three key ideas. First, we advocate a data-driven strategy to generate object masks that serve as a pixel grouping prior for semantic segmentation. This approach omits handcrafted priors, which are often designed for specific scene compositions and limit the applicability of competing frameworks. Second, MaskDistill clusters the object masks to obtain pseudo-ground-truth for training an initial object segmentation model. Third, we leverage this model to filter out low-quality object masks. This strategy mitigates the noise in our pixel grouping prior and results in a clean collection of masks which we use to train a final segmentation model. By combining these components, we can considerably outperform previous works for unsupervised semantic segmentation on PASCAL (+11% mIoU) and COCO (+4% mask AP50). Interestingly, as opposed to existing approaches, our framework does not latch onto low-level image cues and is not limited to object-centric datasets. The code and models will be made available.
abstract_zh: 无监督语义分割的任务旨在将像素聚类成语义上有意义的组。具体来说，分配给同一簇的像素应该共享高级语义属性，例如它们的对象或部分类别。本文提出了 MaskDistill：一种基于三个关键思想的无监督语义分割的新颖框架。首先，我们提倡采用数据驱动策略来生成对象掩模，该对象掩模用作语义分割之前的像素分组。这种方法省略了手工制作的先验，这些先验通常是针对特定场景组合而设计的，并限制了竞争框架的适用性。其次，MaskDistill 对对象掩码进行聚类以获得伪地面实况，用于训练初始对象分割模型。第三，我们利用这个模型来过滤掉低质量的对象蒙版。这种策略减轻了我们之前的像素分组中的噪声，并产生了干净的掩模集合，我们用它来训练最终的分割模型。通过组合这些组件，我们可以大大优于之前在 PASCAL（+11% mIoU）和 COCO（+4% mask AP50）上的无监督语义分割工作。有趣的是，与现有方法相反，我们的框架不会锁定低级图像线索，也不限于以对象为中心的数据集。代码和模型将公开。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于Transformer的无监督语义分割框架MaskDistill，通过生成物体掩码并优化聚类策略实现像素级语义分组。
AI.motivation: 现有无监督分割方法依赖手工设计的先验知识，限制了模型的通用性。本文旨在通过数据驱动策略消除对特定场景先验的依赖，提升模型适应性。
AI.method: 1) 数据驱动生成物体掩码作为像素分组先验；2) 聚类掩码生成伪标签训练初始分割模型；3) 利用模型过滤低质量掩码，迭代优化最终分割模型。
AI.result: 在PASCAL数据集上mIoU提升11%，COCO数据集mask AP50提升4%，且不依赖低层图像特征或特定物体数据集。
AI.conclusion: MaskDistill通过端到端的掩码生成与优化策略，在无监督场景下实现了优于现有方法的分割性能，突破了传统方法对先验知识和数据分布的限制。

search_text: Discovering Object Masks with Transformers for Unsupervised Semantic Segmentation 使用 Transformers 发现对象掩码以进行无监督语义分割 The task of unsupervised semantic segmentation aims to cluster pixels into semantically meaningful groups. Specifically, pixels assigned to the same cluster should share high-level semantic properties like their object or part category. This paper presents MaskDistill: a novel framework for unsupervised semantic segmentation based on three key ideas. First, we advocate a data-driven strategy to generate object masks that serve as a pixel grouping prior for semantic segmentation. This approach omits handcrafted priors, which are often designed for specific scene compositions and limit the applicability of competing frameworks. Second, MaskDistill clusters the object masks to obtain pseudo-ground-truth for training an initial object segmentation model. Third, we leverage this model to filter out low-quality object masks. This strategy mitigates the noise in our pixel grouping prior and results in a clean collection of masks which we use to train a final segmentation model. By combining these components, we can considerably outperform previous works for unsupervised semantic segmentation on PASCAL (+11% mIoU) and COCO (+4% mask AP50). Interestingly, as opposed to existing approaches, our framework does not latch onto low-level image cues and is not limited to object-centric datasets. The code and models will be made available. 无监督语义分割的任务旨在将像素聚类成语义上有意义的组。具体来说，分配给同一簇的像素应该共享高级语义属性，例如它们的对象或部分类别。本文提出了 MaskDistill：一种基于三个关键思想的无监督语义分割的新颖框架。首先，我们提倡采用数据驱动策略来生成对象掩模，该对象掩模用作语义分割之前的像素分组。这种方法省略了手工制作的先验，这些先验通常是针对特定场景组合而设计的，并限制了竞争框架的适用性。其次，MaskDistill 对对象掩码进行聚类以获得伪地面实况，用于训练初始对象分割模型。第三，我们利用这个模型来过滤掉低质量的对象蒙版。这种策略减轻了我们之前的像素分组中的噪声，并产生了干净的掩模集合，我们用它来训练最终的分割模型。通过组合这些组件，我们可以大大优于之前在 PASCAL（+11% mIoU）和 COCO（+4% mask AP50）上的无监督语义分割工作。有趣的是，与现有方法相反，我们的框架不会锁定低级图像线索，也不限于以对象为中心的数据集。代码和模型将公开。 本文提出了一种基于Transformer的无监督语义分割框架MaskDistill，通过生成物体掩码并优化聚类策略实现像素级语义分组。 Wouter Van Gansbeke,Simon Vandenhende,Luc Van Gool cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Kai Zhang,Nick Kolkin,Sai Bi,Fujun Luan,Zexiang Xu,Eli Shechtman,Noah Snavely
title: ARF: Artistic Radiance Fields
title_zh: ARF：艺术光辉场
abstract: We present a method for transferring the artistic features of an arbitrary style image to a 3D scene. Previous methods that perform 3D stylization on point clouds or meshes are sensitive to geometric reconstruction errors for complex real-world scenes. Instead, we propose to stylize the more robust radiance field representation. We find that the commonly used Gram matrix-based loss tends to produce blurry results without faithful brushstrokes, and introduce a nearest neighbor-based loss that is highly effective at capturing style details while maintaining multi-view consistency. We also propose a novel deferred back-propagation method to enable optimization of memory-intensive radiance fields using style losses defined on full-resolution rendered images. Our extensive evaluation demonstrates that our method outperforms baselines by generating artistic appearance that more closely resembles the style image. Please check our project page for video results and open-source implementations: https://www.cs.cornell.edu/projects/arf/ .
summary: We present a method for transferring the artistic features of an arbitrary style image to a 3D scene. Previous methods that perform 3D stylization on point clouds or meshes are sensitive to geometric reconstruction errors for complex real-world scenes. Instead, we propose to stylize the more robust radiance field representation. We find that the commonly used Gram matrix-based loss tends to produce blurry results without faithful brushstrokes, and introduce a nearest neighbor-based loss that is highly effective at capturing style details while maintaining multi-view consistency. We also propose a novel deferred back-propagation method to enable optimization of memory-intensive radiance fields using style losses defined on full-resolution rendered images. Our extensive evaluation demonstrates that our method outperforms baselines by generating artistic appearance that more closely resembles the style image. Please check our project page for video results and open-source implementations: https://www.cs.cornell.edu/projects/arf/ .
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种将任意风格图像的艺术特征转移到 3D 场景的方法。以前对点云或网格执行 3D 风格化的方法对复杂现实场景的几何重建误差很敏感。相反，我们建议对更稳健的辐射场表示进行风格化。我们发现，常用的基于 Gram 矩阵的损失往往会在没有忠实笔触的情况下产生模糊的结果，并且引入了基于最近邻的损失，该损失在捕获风格细节方面非常有效，同时保持多视图一致性。我们还提出了一种新颖的延迟反向传播方法，可以使用在全分辨率渲染图像上定义的样式损失来优化内存密集型辐射场。我们的广泛评估表明，我们的方法通过生成更接近风格图像的艺术外观而优于基线。请查看我们的项目页面以获取视频结果和开源实现：https://www.cs.cornell.edu/projects/arf/。

AI.tldr: 提出一种将任意风格图像的艺术特征转移到3D场景的方法。
AI.motivation: 传统基于点云或网格的3D风格化方法对复杂场景的几何重建误差敏感，而辐射场表示更稳健。
AI.method: 引入基于最近邻的损失函数捕捉风格细节，结合延迟反向传播优化高分辨率渲染图像的风格损失。
AI.result: 生成的艺术效果更贴近目标风格图像，在多视图一致性上优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 辐射场表示结合新型损失函数能有效实现高质量3D艺术风格迁移。

authors_text: Zhuoran Yu,Yin Li,Yong Jae Lee
title: EnergyMatch: Energy-based Pseudo-Labeling for Semi-Supervised Learning
title_zh: EnergyMatch：用于半监督学习的基于能量的伪标签
abstract: Recent state-of-the-art methods in semi-supervised learning (SSL) combine consistency regularization with confidence-based pseudo-labeling. To obtain high-quality pseudo-labels, a high confidence threshold is typically adopted. However, it has been shown that softmax-based confidence scores in deep networks can be arbitrarily high for samples far from the training data, and thus, the pseudo-labels for even high-confidence unlabeled samples may still be unreliable. In this work, we present a new perspective of pseudo-labeling: instead of relying on model confidence, we instead measure whether an unlabeled sample is likely to be "in-distribution"; i.e., close to the current training data. To classify whether an unlabeled sample is "in-distribution" or "out-of-distribution", we adopt the energy score from out-of-distribution detection literature. As training progresses and more unlabeled samples become in-distribution and contribute to training, the combined labeled and pseudo-labeled data can better approximate the true distribution to improve the model. Experiments demonstrate that our energy-based pseudo-labeling method, albeit conceptually simple, significantly outperforms confidence-based methods on imbalanced SSL benchmarks, and achieves competitive performance on class-balanced data. For example, it produces a 4-6% absolute accuracy improvement on CIFAR10-LT when the imbalance ratio is higher than 50. When combined with state-of-the-art long-tailed SSL methods, further improvements are attained.
summary: Recent state-of-the-art methods in semi-supervised learning (SSL) combine consistency regularization with confidence-based pseudo-labeling. To obtain high-quality pseudo-labels, a high confidence threshold is typically adopted. However, it has been shown that softmax-based confidence scores in deep networks can be arbitrarily high for samples far from the training data, and thus, the pseudo-labels for even high-confidence unlabeled samples may still be unreliable. In this work, we present a new perspective of pseudo-labeling: instead of relying on model confidence, we instead measure whether an unlabeled sample is likely to be "in-distribution"; i.e., close to the current training data. To classify whether an unlabeled sample is "in-distribution" or "out-of-distribution", we adopt the energy score from out-of-distribution detection literature. As training progresses and more unlabeled samples become in-distribution and contribute to training, the combined labeled and pseudo-labeled data can better approximate the true distribution to improve the model. Experiments demonstrate that our energy-based pseudo-labeling method, albeit conceptually simple, significantly outperforms confidence-based methods on imbalanced SSL benchmarks, and achieves competitive performance on class-balanced data. For example, it produces a 4-6% absolute accuracy improvement on CIFAR10-LT when the imbalance ratio is higher than 50. When combined with state-of-the-art long-tailed SSL methods, further improvements are attained.
abstract_zh: 最近最先进的半监督学习（SSL）方法将一致性正则化与基于置信度的伪标签相结合。为了获得高质量的伪标签，通常采用高置信度阈值。然而，事实证明，对于远离训练数据的样本，深度网络中基于 softmax 的置信度分数可能任意高，因此，即使是高置信度未标记样本的伪标签也可能仍然不可靠。在这项工作中，我们提出了伪标记的新视角：我们不依赖模型置信度，而是衡量未标记样本是否可能“分布内”；即接近当前的训练数据。为了对未标记样本是“分布内”还是“分布外”进行分类，我们采用分布外检测文献中的能量得分。随着训练的进行，更多未标记的样本变得分布均匀并有助于训练，组合的标记和伪标记数据可以更好地逼近真实分布以改进模型。实验表明，我们的基于能量的伪标记方法虽然概念上很简单，但在不平衡 SSL 基准上显着优于基于置信度的方法，并且在类平衡数据上实现了有竞争力的性能。例如，当不平衡比高于 50 时，它在 CIFAR10-LT 上的绝对精度提高了 4-6%。当与最先进的长尾 SSL 方法相结合时，可以获得进一步的改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于能量得分的伪标签方法，用于半监督学习中的伪标签生成，以提高模型性能。
AI.motivation: 现有基于置信度的伪标签方法在处理远离训练数据的样本时可能产生不可靠的标签，而能量得分能更准确判断样本是否属于分布内数据。
AI.method: 采用能量得分替代置信度阈值，通过检测未标记样本是否为分布内数据生成伪标签，并结合一致性正则化进行训练。
AI.result: 在不平衡数据集CIFAR10-LT上实现4-6%的绝对精度提升，且在类别平衡数据上表现与现有方法相当。
AI.conclusion: 能量得分伪标签方法在概念简单的情况下显著优于置信度方法，且可与其他长尾SSL方法结合进一步提升效果。

tldr: 本文提出了一种基于能量得分的伪标签方法，用于半监督学习中的伪标签生成，以提高模型性能。
motivation: 现有基于置信度的伪标签方法在处理远离训练数据的样本时可能产生不可靠的标签，而能量得分能更准确判断样本是否属于分布内数据。
method: 采用能量得分替代置信度阈值，通过检测未标记样本是否为分布内数据生成伪标签，并结合一致性正则化进行训练。
result: 在不平衡数据集CIFAR10-LT上实现4-6%的绝对精度提升，且在类别平衡数据上表现与现有方法相当。
conclusion: 能量得分伪标签方法在概念简单的情况下显著优于置信度方法，且可与其他长尾SSL方法结合进一步提升效果。
search_text: EnergyMatch: Energy-based Pseudo-Labeling for Semi-Supervised Learning EnergyMatch：用于半监督学习的基于能量的伪标签 Recent state-of-the-art methods in semi-supervised learning (SSL) combine consistency regularization with confidence-based pseudo-labeling. To obtain high-quality pseudo-labels, a high confidence threshold is typically adopted. However, it has been shown that softmax-based confidence scores in deep networks can be arbitrarily high for samples far from the training data, and thus, the pseudo-labels for even high-confidence unlabeled samples may still be unreliable. In this work, we present a new perspective of pseudo-labeling: instead of relying on model confidence, we instead measure whether an unlabeled sample is likely to be "in-distribution"; i.e., close to the current training data. To classify whether an unlabeled sample is "in-distribution" or "out-of-distribution", we adopt the energy score from out-of-distribution detection literature. As training progresses and more unlabeled samples become in-distribution and contribute to training, the combined labeled and pseudo-labeled data can better approximate the true distribution to improve the model. Experiments demonstrate that our energy-based pseudo-labeling method, albeit conceptually simple, significantly outperforms confidence-based methods on imbalanced SSL benchmarks, and achieves competitive performance on class-balanced data. For example, it produces a 4-6% absolute accuracy improvement on CIFAR10-LT when the imbalance ratio is higher than 50. When combined with state-of-the-art long-tailed SSL methods, further improvements are attained. 最近最先进的半监督学习（SSL）方法将一致性正则化与基于置信度的伪标签相结合。为了获得高质量的伪标签，通常采用高置信度阈值。然而，事实证明，对于远离训练数据的样本，深度网络中基于 softmax 的置信度分数可能任意高，因此，即使是高置信度未标记样本的伪标签也可能仍然不可靠。在这项工作中，我们提出了伪标记的新视角：我们不依赖模型置信度，而是衡量未标记样本是否可能“分布内”；即接近当前的训练数据。为了对未标记样本是“分布内”还是“分布外”进行分类，我们采用分布外检测文献中的能量得分。随着训练的进行，更多未标记的样本变得分布均匀并有助于训练，组合的标记和伪标记数据可以更好地逼近真实分布以改进模型。实验表明，我们的基于能量的伪标记方法虽然概念上很简单，但在不平衡 SSL 基准上显着优于基于置信度的方法，并且在类平衡数据上实现了有竞争力的性能。例如，当不平衡比高于 50 时，它在 CIFAR10-LT 上的绝对精度提高了 4-6%。当与最先进的长尾 SSL 方法相结合时，可以获得进一步的改进。 本文提出了一种基于能量得分的伪标签方法，用于半监督学习中的伪标签生成，以提高模型性能。 Zhuoran Yu,Yin Li,Yong Jae Lee cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Mustafa Abdallah,Byung-Gun Joung,Wo Jae Lee,Charilaos Mousoulis,John W. Sutherland,Saurabh Bagchi
title: Anomaly Detection and Inter-Sensor Transfer Learning on Smart Manufacturing Datasets
title_zh: 智能制造数据集的异常检测和传感器间迁移学习
comment: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2102.05814
comments: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2102.05814
abstract: Smart manufacturing systems are being deployed at a growing rate because of their ability to interpret a wide variety of sensed information and act on the knowledge gleaned from system observations. In many cases, the principal goal of the smart manufacturing system is to rapidly detect (or anticipate) failures to reduce operational cost and eliminate downtime. This often boils down to detecting anomalies within the sensor date acquired from the system. The smart manufacturing application domain poses certain salient technical challenges. In particular, there are often multiple types of sensors with varying capabilities and costs. The sensor data characteristics change with the operating point of the environment or machines, such as, the RPM of the motor. The anomaly detection process therefore has to be calibrated near an operating point. In this paper, we analyze four datasets from sensors deployed from manufacturing testbeds. We evaluate the performance of several traditional and ML-based forecasting models for predicting the time series of sensor data. Then, considering the sparse data from one kind of sensor, we perform transfer learning from a high data rate sensor to perform defect type classification. Taken together, we show that predictive failure classification can be achieved, thus paving the way for predictive maintenance.
summary: Smart manufacturing systems are being deployed at a growing rate because of their ability to interpret a wide variety of sensed information and act on the knowledge gleaned from system observations. In many cases, the principal goal of the smart manufacturing system is to rapidly detect (or anticipate) failures to reduce operational cost and eliminate downtime. This often boils down to detecting anomalies within the sensor date acquired from the system. The smart manufacturing application domain poses certain salient technical challenges. In particular, there are often multiple types of sensors with varying capabilities and costs. The sensor data characteristics change with the operating point of the environment or machines, such as, the RPM of the motor. The anomaly detection process therefore has to be calibrated near an operating point. In this paper, we analyze four datasets from sensors deployed from manufacturing testbeds. We evaluate the performance of several traditional and ML-based forecasting models for predicting the time series of sensor data. Then, considering the sparse data from one kind of sensor, we perform transfer learning from a high data rate sensor to perform defect type classification. Taken together, we show that predictive failure classification can be achieved, thus paving the way for predictive maintenance.
abstract_zh: 智能制造系统的部署速度越来越快，因为它们能够解释各种感知信息并根据从系统观察中收集的知识采取行动。在许多情况下，智能制造系统的主要目标是快速检测（或预测）故障，以降低运营成本并消除停机时间。这通常归结为检测从系统获取的传感器数据中的异常情况。智能制造应用领域提出了一些突出的技术挑战。特别是，通常有多种类型的传感器，具有不同的功能和成本。传感器数据特性随着环境或机器的工作点而变化，例如电机的转速。因此，异常检测过程必须在工作点附近进行校准。在本文中，我们分析了制造测试台部署的传感器的四个数据集。我们评估了几种传统和基于机器学习的预测模型在预测传感器数据时间序列方面的性能。然后，考虑到来自一种传感器的稀疏数据，我们从高数据速率传感器执行迁移学习以执行缺陷类型分类。总而言之，我们表明可以实现预测性故障分类，从而为预测性维护铺平道路。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过异常检测和跨传感器迁移学习方法，解决智能制造业中的故障预测问题，以实现预测性维护。
AI.motivation: 智能制造业中传感器数据随运行状态变化且部分传感器数据稀疏，导致传统方法难以准确检测异常，需通过迁移学习提升缺陷分类的准确性。
AI.method: 采用传统时间序列预测模型与机器学习方法评估传感器数据预测性能，并通过迁移学习将高数据率传感器的知识迁移至低数据率传感器，实现缺陷分类。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，预测性故障分类可行，有效提升了预测性维护的实现能力。
AI.conclusion: 跨传感器迁移学习能有效解决数据稀疏问题，为智能制造业的预测性维护提供了可行的解决方案。

authors_text: Elad Ben-Avraham,Roei Herzig,Karttikeya Mangalam,Amir Bar,Anna Rohrbach,Leonid Karlinsky,Trevor Darrell,Amir Globerson
title: Bringing Image Scene Structure to Video via Frame-Clip Consistency of Object Tokens
title_zh: 通过对象标记的帧剪辑一致性将图像场景结构引入视频
abstract: Recent action recognition models have achieved impressive results by integrating objects, their locations and interactions. However, obtaining dense structured annotations for each frame is tedious and time-consuming, making these methods expensive to train and less scalable. At the same time, if a small set of annotated images is available, either within or outside the domain of interest, how could we leverage these for a video downstream task? We propose a learning framework StructureViT (SViT for short), which demonstrates how utilizing the structure of a small number of images only available during training can improve a video model. SViT relies on two key insights. First, as both images and videos contain structured information, we enrich a transformer model with a set of \emph{object tokens} that can be used across images and videos. Second, the scene representations of individual frames in video should "align" with those of still images. This is achieved via a \emph{Frame-Clip Consistency} loss, which ensures the flow of structured information between images and videos. We explore a particular instantiation of scene structure, namely a \emph{Hand-Object Graph}, consisting of hands and objects with their locations as nodes, and physical relations of contact/no-contact as edges. SViT shows strong performance improvements on multiple video understanding tasks and datasets. Furthermore, it won in the Ego4D CVPR'22 Object State Localization challenge. For code and pretrained models, visit the project page at \url{https://eladb3.github.io/SViT/}
summary: Recent action recognition models have achieved impressive results by integrating objects, their locations and interactions. However, obtaining dense structured annotations for each frame is tedious and time-consuming, making these methods expensive to train and less scalable. At the same time, if a small set of annotated images is available, either within or outside the domain of interest, how could we leverage these for a video downstream task? We propose a learning framework StructureViT (SViT for short), which demonstrates how utilizing the structure of a small number of images only available during training can improve a video model. SViT relies on two key insights. First, as both images and videos contain structured information, we enrich a transformer model with a set of \emph{object tokens} that can be used across images and videos. Second, the scene representations of individual frames in video should "align" with those of still images. This is achieved via a \emph{Frame-Clip Consistency} loss, which ensures the flow of structured information between images and videos. We explore a particular instantiation of scene structure, namely a \emph{Hand-Object Graph}, consisting of hands and objects with their locations as nodes, and physical relations of contact/no-contact as edges. SViT shows strong performance improvements on multiple video understanding tasks and datasets. Furthermore, it won in the Ego4D CVPR'22 Object State Localization challenge. For code and pretrained models, visit the project page at \url{https://eladb3.github.io/SViT/}
abstract_zh: 最近的动作识别模型通过整合对象、它们的位置和交互取得了令人印象深刻的结果。然而，为每个帧获取密集的结构化注释是乏味且耗时的，使得这些方法的训练成本昂贵并且可扩展性较差。同时，如果一小组带注释的图像可用，无论是在感兴趣的领域之内还是之外，我们如何利用这些图像来执行视频下游任务？我们提出了一个学习框架 StructureViT（简称 SViT），它演示了如何利用仅在训练期间可用的少量图像的结构来改进视频模型。 SViT 依赖于两个关键见解。首先，由于图像和视频都包含结构化信息，我们使用一组可在图像和视频之间使用的 \emph{object tokens} 来丰富 Transformer 模型。其次，视频中各个帧的场景表示应与静态图像的场景表示“对齐”。这是通过 \emph{Frame-Clip Consistency} 损失来实现的，它确保了图像和视频之间的结构化信息的流动。我们探索场景结构的特定实例，即 \emph{Hand-Object Graph}，由手和物体组成，其位置作为节点，接触/非接触的物理关系作为边缘。 SViT 在多个视频理解任务和数据集上显示出强大的性能改进。此外，它还在 Ego4D CVPR'22 对象状态定位挑战赛中获胜。有关代码和预训练模型，请访问项目页面 \url{https://eladb3.github.io/SViT/}

AI.tldr: 本文提出StructureViT（SViT）框架，通过图像场景结构的帧-片段一致性提升视频理解任务性能。
AI.motivation: 现有视频动作识别模型依赖密集标注数据，获取成本高且扩展性差；如何利用少量标注图像提升视频任务性能是关键问题。
AI.method: 设计包含对象标记的Transformer模型，通过帧-片段一致性损失对齐视频帧与静态图像的场景表征，并以手-物体图作为场景结构实例化方案。
AI.result: 在多个视频理解数据集上取得显著性能提升，并在Ego4D CVPR'22物体状态定位挑战赛中夺冠。
AI.conclusion: SViT通过跨模态结构对齐有效利用图像标注数据，为视频模型训练提供可扩展的解决方案。

Second, the scene representations of individual frames in video should "align" with those of still images. This is achieved via a \emph{Frame-Clip Consistency} loss, which ensures the flow of structured information between images and videos. We explore a particular instantiation of scene structure, namely a \emph{Hand-Object Graph}, consisting of hands and objects with their locations as nodes, and physical relations of contact/no-contact as edges. SViT shows strong performance improvements on multiple video understanding tasks and datasets. Furthermore, it won in the Ego4D CVPR'22 Object State Localization challenge. For code and pretrained models, visit the project page at \url{https://eladb3.github.io/SViT/} 最近的动作识别模型通过整合对象、它们的位置和交互取得了令人印象深刻的结果。然而，为每个帧获取密集的结构化注释是乏味且耗时的，使得这些方法的训练成本昂贵并且可扩展性较差。同时，如果一小组带注释的图像可用，无论是在感兴趣的领域之内还是之外，我们如何利用这些图像来执行视频下游任务？我们提出了一个学习框架 StructureViT（简称 SViT），它演示了如何利用仅在训练期间可用的少量图像的结构来改进视频模型。 SViT 依赖于两个关键见解。首先，由于图像和视频都包含结构化信息，我们使用一组可在图像和视频之间使用的 \emph{object tokens} 来丰富 Transformer 模型。其次，视频中各个帧的场景表示应与静态图像的场景表示“对齐”。这是通过 \emph{Frame-Clip Consistency} 损失来实现的，它确保了图像和视频之间的结构化信息的流动。我们探索场景结构的特定实例，即 \emph{Hand-Object Graph}，由手和物体组成，其位置作为节点，接触/非接触的物理关系作为边缘。 SViT 在多个视频理解任务和数据集上显示出强大的性能改进。此外，它还在 Ego4D CVPR'22 对象状态定位挑战赛中获胜。有关代码和预训练模型，请访问项目页面 \url{https://eladb3.github.io/SViT/} 本文提出StructureViT（SViT）框架，通过图像场景结构的帧-片段一致性提升视频理解任务性能。 Elad Ben-Avraham,Roei Herzig,Karttikeya Mangalam,Amir Bar,Anna Rohrbach,Leonid Karlinsky,Trevor Darrell,Amir Globerson cs.CV

authors_text: Xueqi Guo,Bo Zhou,David Pigg,Bruce Spottiswoode,Michael E. Casey,Chi Liu,Nicha C. Dvornek
title: Unsupervised inter-frame motion correction for whole-body dynamic PET using convolutional long short-term memory in a convolutional neural network
title_zh: 使用卷积神经网络中的卷积长短期记忆对全身动态 PET 进行无监督帧间运动校正
comment: Preprint submitted to Medical Image Analysis
comments: Preprint submitted to Medical Image Analysis
abstract: Subject motion in whole-body dynamic PET introduces inter-frame mismatch and seriously impacts parametric imaging. Traditional non-rigid registration methods are generally computationally intense and time-consuming. Deep learning approaches are promising in achieving high accuracy with fast speed, but have yet been investigated with consideration for tracer distribution changes or in the whole-body scope. In this work, we developed an unsupervised automatic deep learning-based framework to correct inter-frame body motion. The motion estimation network is a convolutional neural network with a combined convolutional long short-term memory layer, fully utilizing dynamic temporal features and spatial information. Our dataset contains 27 subjects each under a 90-min FDG whole-body dynamic PET scan. With 9-fold cross-validation, compared with both traditional and deep learning baselines, we demonstrated that the proposed network obtained superior performance in enhanced qualitative and quantitative spatial alignment between parametric $K_{i}$ and $V_{b}$ images and in significantly reduced parametric fitting error. We also showed the potential of the proposed motion correction method for impacting downstream analysis of the estimated parametric images, improving the ability to distinguish malignant from benign hypermetabolic regions of interest. Once trained, the motion estimation inference time of our proposed network was around 460 times faster than the conventional registration baseline, showing its potential to be easily applied in clinical settings.

Our dataset contains 27 subjects each under a 90-min FDG whole-body dynamic PET scan. With 9-fold cross-validation, compared with both traditional and deep learning baselines, we demonstrated that the proposed network obtained superior performance in enhanced qualitative and quantitative spatial alignment between parametric $K_{i}$ and $V_{b}$ images and in significantly reduced parametric fitting error. We also showed the potential of the proposed motion correction method for impacting downstream analysis of the estimated parametric images, improving the ability to distinguish malignant from benign hypermetabolic regions of interest. Once trained, the motion estimation inference time of our proposed network was around 460 times faster than the conventional registration baseline, showing its potential to be easily applied in clinical settings.
abstract_zh: 全身动态 PET 中的主体运动会引入帧间失配并严重影响参数成像。传统的非刚性配准方法通常计算量大且耗时。深度学习方法有望快速实现高精度，但尚未在考虑示踪剂分布变化或全身范围的情况下进行研究。在这项工作中，我们开发了一种基于无监督自动深度学习的框架来纠正帧间身体运动。运动估计网络是具有组合卷积长短期记忆层的卷积神经网络，充分利用动态时间特征和空间信息。我们的数据集包含 27 名受试者，每名受试者都接受了 90 分钟的 FDG 全身动态 PET 扫描。通过 9 倍交叉验证，与传统和深度学习基线相比，我们证明所提出的网络在增强参数 $K_{i}$ 和 $V_{b}$ 图像之间的定性和定量空间对齐方面获得了优越的性能，并显着降低了参数拟合误差。我们还展示了所提出的运动校正方法对影响估计参数图像的下游分析的潜力，提高了区分恶性和良性代谢亢进区域的能力。经过训练后，我们提出的网络的运动估计推理时间比传统配准基线快约 460 倍，显示出其在临床环境中轻松应用的潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于卷积长短期记忆网络的无监督深度学习框架，用于校正全身动态PET成像中的帧间运动。
AI.motivation: 传统非刚性配准方法计算量大且耗时，现有深度学习方法未充分考虑示踪剂分布变化或全身范围的运动校正需求。
AI.method: 构建包含卷积长短期记忆层的卷积神经网络，融合时空特征；使用27例90分钟FDG全身动态PET扫描数据进行9折交叉验证。
AI.result: 相较传统方法和深度学习基线，参数图像空间对齐度提升，参数拟合误差显著降低，推理速度提升460倍，并增强良恶性高代谢区域区分能力。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在保持高精度的同时显著提升效率，具有临床应用潜力，可优化动态PET参数图像的下游分析。

authors_text: Eldar Insafutdinov,Dylan Campbell,João F. Henriques,Andrea Vedaldi
title: SNeS: Learning Probably Symmetric Neural Surfaces from Incomplete Data
title_zh: SNeS：从不完整的数据中学习可能对称的神经表面
abstract: We present a method for the accurate 3D reconstruction of partly-symmetric objects. We build on the strengths of recent advances in neural reconstruction and rendering such as Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF). A major shortcoming of such approaches is that they fail to reconstruct any part of the object which is not clearly visible in the training image, which is often the case for in-the-wild images and videos. When evidence is lacking, structural priors such as symmetry can be used to complete the missing information. However, exploiting such priors in neural rendering is highly non-trivial: while geometry and non-reflective materials may be symmetric, shadows and reflections from the ambient scene are not symmetric in general. To address this, we apply a soft symmetry constraint to the 3D geometry and material properties, having factored appearance into lighting, albedo colour and reflectivity. We evaluate our method on the recently introduced CO3D dataset, focusing on the car category due to the challenge of reconstructing highly-reflective materials. We show that it can reconstruct unobserved regions with high fidelity and render high-quality novel view images.
summary: We present a method for the accurate 3D reconstruction of partly-symmetric objects. We build on the strengths of recent advances in neural reconstruction and rendering such as Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF). A major shortcoming of such approaches is that they fail to reconstruct any part of the object which is not clearly visible in the training image, which is often the case for in-the-wild images and videos. When evidence is lacking, structural priors such as symmetry can be used to complete the missing information. However, exploiting such priors in neural rendering is highly non-trivial: while geometry and non-reflective materials may be symmetric, shadows and reflections from the ambient scene are not symmetric in general. To address this, we apply a soft symmetry constraint to the 3D geometry and material properties, having factored appearance into lighting, albedo colour and reflectivity. We evaluate our method on the recently introduced CO3D dataset, focusing on the car category due to the challenge of reconstructing highly-reflective materials. We show that it can reconstruct unobserved regions with high fidelity and render high-quality novel view images.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种部分对称物体的精确 3D 重建方法。我们利用神经重建和渲染方面最新进展的优势，例如神经辐射场 (NeRF)。这种方法的一个主要缺点是它们无法重建训练图像中不清晰可见的物体的任何部分，这对于野外图像和视频来说通常是这种情况。当缺乏证据时，可以使用对称性等结构先验来补充缺失的信息。然而，在神经渲染中利用此类先验是非常重要的：虽然几何形状和非反射材料可能是对称的，但来自环境场景的阴影和反射通常不是对称的。为了解决这个问题，我们对 3D 几何和材料属性应用了软对称约束，将外观纳入照明、反照率颜色和反射率中。我们在最近引入的 CO3D 数据集上评估我们的方法，由于重建高反射材料的挑战，重点关注汽车类别。我们证明它可以以高保真度重建未观察到的区域并渲染高质量的新颖视图图像。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于对称性先验的神经表面学习方法，用于从不完整数据中重建部分对称物体的高质量3D模型。
AI.motivation: 现有神经渲染方法（如NeRF）难以重建训练图像中不可见区域，而对称性作为结构先验可补充缺失信息，但如何在考虑光照反射非对称性的情况下有效应用该先验仍是挑战。
AI.method: 通过软对称约束分离3D几何与材质属性，将外观分解为光照、反照率和反射率，结合神经表面学习实现对称性引导的缺失区域重建。
AI.result: 在CO3D数据集汽车类别测试中，方法成功重建未观测区域并生成高质量新视角图像，尤其在处理高反射材料时表现优异。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过软对称约束有效融合结构先验与神经渲染，解决了不完整数据下对称物体的高质量重建问题。

To address this, we apply a soft symmetry constraint to the 3D geometry and material properties, having factored appearance into lighting, albedo colour and reflectivity. We evaluate our method on the recently introduced CO3D dataset, focusing on the car category due to the challenge of reconstructing highly-reflective materials. We show that it can reconstruct unobserved regions with high fidelity and render high-quality novel view images. 我们提出了一种部分对称物体的精确 3D 重建方法。我们利用神经重建和渲染方面最新进展的优势，例如神经辐射场 (NeRF)。这种方法的一个主要缺点是它们无法重建训练图像中不清晰可见的物体的任何部分，这对于野外图像和视频来说通常是这种情况。当缺乏证据时，可以使用对称性等结构先验来补充缺失的信息。然而，在神经渲染中利用此类先验是非常重要的：虽然几何形状和非反射材料可能是对称的，但来自环境场景的阴影和反射通常不是对称的。为了解决这个问题，我们对 3D 几何和材料属性应用了软对称约束，将外观纳入照明、反照率颜色和反射率中。我们在最近引入的 CO3D 数据集上评估我们的方法，由于重建高反射材料的挑战，重点关注汽车类别。我们证明它可以以高保真度重建未观察到的区域并渲染高质量的新颖视图图像。 本文提出一种基于对称性先验的神经表面学习方法，用于从不完整数据中重建部分对称物体的高质量3D模型。 Eldar Insafutdinov,Dylan Campbell,João F. Henriques,Andrea Vedaldi cs.CV

authors_text: Luciano Miuccio,Salvatore Riolo,Sumudu Samarakoon,Daniela Panno,Mehdi Bennis
title: Learning Generalized Wireless MAC Communication Protocols via Abstraction
title_zh: 通过抽象学习通用无线 MAC 通信协议
abstract: To tackle the heterogeneous requirements of beyond 5G (B5G) and future 6G wireless networks, conventional medium access control (MAC) procedures need to evolve to enable base stations (BSs) and user equipments (UEs) to automatically learn innovative MAC protocols catering to extremely diverse services. This topic has received significant attention, and several reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms, in which BSs and UEs are cast as agents, are available with the aim of learning a communication policy based on agents' local observations. However, current approaches are typically overfitted to the environment they are trained in, and lack robustness against unseen conditions, failing to generalize in different environments. To overcome this problem, in this work, instead of learning a policy in the high dimensional and redundant observation space, we leverage the concept of observation abstraction (OA) rooted in extracting useful information from the environment. This in turn allows learning communication protocols that are more robust and with much better generalization capabilities than current baselines. To learn the abstracted information from observations, we propose an architecture based on autoencoder (AE) and imbue it into a multi-agent proximal policy optimization (MAPPO) framework. Simulation results corroborate the effectiveness of leveraging abstraction when learning protocols by generalizing across environments, in terms of number of UEs, number of data packets to transmit, and channel conditions.
summary: To tackle the heterogeneous requirements of beyond 5G (B5G) and future 6G wireless networks, conventional medium access control (MAC) procedures need to evolve to enable base stations (BSs) and user equipments (UEs) to automatically learn innovative MAC protocols catering to extremely diverse services. This topic has received significant attention, and several reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms, in which BSs and UEs are cast as agents, are available with the aim of learning a communication policy based on agents' local observations. However, current approaches are typically overfitted to the environment they are trained in, and lack robustness against unseen conditions, failing to generalize in different environments. To overcome this problem, in this work, instead of learning a policy in the high dimensional and redundant observation space, we leverage the concept of observation abstraction (OA) rooted in extracting useful information from the environment. This in turn allows learning communication protocols that are more robust and with much better generalization capabilities than current baselines. To learn the abstracted information from observations, we propose an architecture based on autoencoder (AE) and imbue it into a multi-agent proximal policy optimization (MAPPO) framework. Simulation results corroborate the effectiveness of leveraging abstraction when learning protocols by generalizing across environments, in terms of number of UEs, number of data packets to transmit, and channel conditions.
abstract_zh: 为了满足超 5G (B5G) 和未来 6G 无线网络的异构要求，传统的介质访问控制 (MAC) 程序需要不断发展，以使基站 (BS) 和用户设备 (UE) 能够自动学习创新的 MAC 协议，以满足极其多样化的服务。该主题受到了广泛关注，并且有几种强化学习（RL）算法可供使用，其中 BS 和 UE 被视为代理，其目的是根据代理的本地观察来学习通信策略。然而，当前的方法通常过度适应它们所训练的环境，并且缺乏针对未见条件的鲁棒性，无法在不同的环境中泛化。为了克服这个问题，在这项工作中，我们不是在高维和冗余观察空间中学习策略，而是利用植根于从环境中提取有用信息的观察抽象（OA）的概念。这反过来又允许学习比当前基线更强大且具有更好泛化能力的通信协议。为了从观察中学习抽象信息，我们提出了一种基于自动编码器（AE）的架构，并将其注入多智能体近端策略优化（MAPPO）框架中。仿真结果证实了在学习协议时通过在 UE 数量、要传输的数据包数量和信道条件方面进行跨环境泛化来学习协议时利用抽象的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过观察抽象技术学习适用于不同环境的通用无线MAC通信协议，以解决现有强化学习方法在异构5G/6G网络中泛化能力不足的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统基于强化学习的MAC协议学习方法过度依赖训练环境，缺乏对未知场景的鲁棒性，难以满足未来6G网络多样化服务需求。
AI.method: 提出结合自编码器（AE）的观察抽象框架，将抽象信息嵌入多智能体近端策略优化（MAPPO）架构，降低高维观测空间冗余度。
AI.result: 仿真表明，该方法在用户设备数量、数据包量及信道条件变化的多场景下，均展现出优于基线方法的协议泛化性能。
AI.conclusion: 观察抽象技术有效提升了MAC协议学习的环境适应性，为构建面向6G的自适应无线通信系统提供了新思路。

authors_text: Michael Yang
title: Visual Transformer for Object Detection
title_zh: 用于物体检测的视觉转换器
comment: In preparation for short paper of conferences. I am using the name Michael Yang
comments: In preparation for short paper of conferences. I am using the name Michael Yang
abstract: Convolutional Neural networks (CNN) have been the first choice of paradigm in many computer vision applications. The convolution operation however has a significant weakness which is it only operates on a local neighborhood of pixels, thus it misses global information of the surrounding neighbors. Transformers, or Self-attention networks to be more specific, on the other hand, have emerged as a recent advance to capture long range interactions of the input, but they have mostly been applied to sequence modeling tasks such as Neural Machine Translation, Image captioning and other Natural Language Processing tasks. Transformers has been applied to natural language related tasks and achieved promising results. However, its applications in visual related tasks are far from being satisfying. Taking into consideration of both the weaknesses of Convolutional Neural Networks and those of the Transformers, in this paper, we consider the use of self-attention for discriminative visual tasks, object detection, as an alternative to convolutions. In this paper, we propose our model: DetTransNet. Extensive experiments show that our model leads to consistent improvements in object detection on COCO across many different models and scales, including ResNets, while keeping the number of parameters similar. In particular, our method achieves a 1.2% Average Precision improvement on COCO object detection task over other baseline models.
summary: Convolutional Neural networks (CNN) have been the first choice of paradigm in many computer vision applications. The convolution operation however has a significant weakness which is it only operates on a local neighborhood of pixels, thus it misses global information of the surrounding neighbors. Transformers, or Self-attention networks to be more specific, on the other hand, have emerged as a recent advance to capture long range interactions of the input, but they have mostly been applied to sequence modeling tasks such as Neural Machine Translation, Image captioning and other Natural Language Processing tasks. Transformers has been applied to natural language related tasks and achieved promising results. However, its applications in visual related tasks are far from being satisfying. Taking into consideration of both the weaknesses of Convolutional Neural Networks and those of the Transformers, in this paper, we consider the use of self-attention for discriminative visual tasks, object detection, as an alternative to convolutions. In this paper, we propose our model: DetTransNet. Extensive experiments show that our model leads to consistent improvements in object detection on COCO across many different models and scales, including ResNets, while keeping the number of parameters similar. In particular, our method achieves a 1.2% Average Precision improvement on COCO object detection task over other baseline models.
abstract_zh: 卷积神经网络（CNN）已成为许多计算机视觉应用中的首选范例。然而，卷积运算有一个明显的弱点，即它仅对像素的局部邻域进行操作，因此它会丢失周围邻域的全局信息。另一方面，Transformers，或者更具体地说是自注意力网络，是捕获输入的长距离交互的最新进展，但它们主要应用于序列建模任务，例如神经机器翻译、图像字幕和其他自然语言处理任务。 Transformers 已应用于自然语言相关任务并取得了可喜的结果。然而，它在视觉相关任务中的应用还远远不能令人满意。考虑到卷积神经网络和变形金刚的弱点，在本文中，我们考虑将自注意力用于判别性视觉任务、目标检测，作为卷积的替代方案。在本文中，我们提出了我们的模型：DetTransNet。大量实验表明，我们的模型可以在包括 ResNet 在内的许多不同模型和尺度上实现 COCO 上目标检测的一致改进，同时保持参数数量相似。特别是，我们的方法在 COCO 目标检测任务上比其他基线模型实现了 1.2% 的平均精度提高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于视觉Transformer的物体检测模型DetTransNet，用于替代传统卷积神经网络（CNN）。
AI.motivation: 卷积神经网络（CNN）存在局部感知局限性，而Transformer在视觉任务中的应用效果尚未达到理想状态，需探索自注意力机制在物体检测中的潜力。
AI.method: 设计DetTransNet模型，将自注意力机制作为卷积的替代方案，通过全局交互捕获长距离依赖关系，并在不同规模模型（如ResNet）上进行验证。
AI.result: 在COCO数据集上，DetTransNet在保持参数量相近的情况下，相比基线模型实现1.2%的平均精度（AP）提升。
AI.conclusion: 自注意力机制能有效增强物体检测性能，证明了Transformer在视觉任务中的可行性与优势。

authors_text: Ramit Sawhney,Shivam Agarwal,Vivek Mittal,Paolo Rosso,Vikram Nanda,Sudheer Chava
title: Cryptocurrency Bubble Detection: A New Stock Market Dataset, Financial Task & Hyperbolic Models
title_zh: 加密货币泡沫检测：新的股市数据集、金融任务和双曲线模型
comment: Proceedings of the 2022 Conference of the North American Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics: Human Language Technologies
comments: Proceedings of the 2022 Conference of the North American Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics: Human Language Technologies
abstract: The rapid spread of information over social media influences quantitative trading and investments. The growing popularity of speculative trading of highly volatile assets such as cryptocurrencies and meme stocks presents a fresh challenge in the financial realm. Investigating such "bubbles" - periods of sudden anomalous behavior of markets are critical in better understanding investor behavior and market dynamics. However, high volatility coupled with massive volumes of chaotic social media texts, especially for underexplored assets like cryptocoins pose a challenge to existing methods. Taking the first step towards NLP for cryptocoins, we present and publicly release CryptoBubbles, a novel multi-span identification task for bubble detection, and a dataset of more than 400 cryptocoins from 9 exchanges over five years spanning over two million tweets. Further, we develop a set of sequence-to-sequence hyperbolic models suited to this multi-span identification task based on the power-law dynamics of cryptocurrencies and user behavior on social media. We further test the effectiveness of our models under zero-shot settings on a test set of Reddit posts pertaining to 29 "meme stocks", which see an increase in trade volume due to social media hype. Through quantitative, qualitative, and zero-shot analyses on Reddit and Twitter spanning cryptocoins and meme-stocks, we show the practical applicability of CryptoBubbles and hyperbolic models.

The growing popularity of speculative trading of highly volatile assets such as cryptocurrencies and meme stocks presents a fresh challenge in the financial realm. Investigating such "bubbles" - periods of sudden anomalous behavior of markets are critical in better understanding investor behavior and market dynamics. However, high volatility coupled with massive volumes of chaotic social media texts, especially for underexplored assets like cryptocoins pose a challenge to existing methods. Taking the first step towards NLP for cryptocoins, we present and publicly release CryptoBubbles, a novel multi-span identification task for bubble detection, and a dataset of more than 400 cryptocoins from 9 exchanges over five years spanning over two million tweets. Further, we develop a set of sequence-to-sequence hyperbolic models suited to this multi-span identification task based on the power-law dynamics of cryptocurrencies and user behavior on social media. We further test the effectiveness of our models under zero-shot settings on a test set of Reddit posts pertaining to 29 "meme stocks", which see an increase in trade volume due to social media hype. Through quantitative, qualitative, and zero-shot analyses on Reddit and Twitter spanning cryptocoins and meme-stocks, we show the practical applicability of CryptoBubbles and hyperbolic models.
abstract_zh: 社交媒体上信息的快速传播影响着量化交易和投资。加密货币和迷因股票等高波动性资产的投机交易日益流行，给金融领域带来了新的挑战。研究这种“泡沫”——市场突然出现异常行为的时期对于更好地理解投资者行为和市场动态至关重要。然而，高波动性加上大量混乱的社交媒体文本，特别是对于加密货币等未经充分开发的资产，对现有方法构成了挑战。我们向加密货币 NLP 迈出了第一步，提出并公开发布了 CryptoBubbles，这是一种用于泡沫检测的新型多跨度识别任务，以及五年内来自 9 个交易所的 400 多种加密货币的数据集，涵盖超过 200 万条推文。此外，我们基于加密货币的幂律动力学和社交媒体上的用户行为，开发了一组适合这种多跨度识别任务的序列到序列双曲模型。我们在与 29 只“模因股票”相关的 Reddit 帖子测试集上进一步测试了我们的模型在零样本设置下的有效性，这些股票的交易量由于社交媒体炒作而增加。通过对 Reddit 和 Twitter 上的加密货币和模因股票进行定量、定性和零样本分析，我们展示了 CryptoBubbles 和双曲线模型的实际适用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新的加密货币泡沫检测方法，结合多跨度识别任务和基于超双曲模型的序列到序列模型，并构建了包含400多种加密货币数据集CryptoBubbles。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以应对加密货币和迷因股等高波动性资产在社交媒体影响下的泡沫检测问题，需处理海量非结构化文本和复杂市场动态。
AI.method: 构建包含两年多推文数据的CryptoBubbles数据集，开发基于幂律动态的超双曲序列模型，并通过Reddit迷因股数据进行零样本测试。
AI.result: 模型在Twitter加密货币和Reddit迷因股数据上均表现出色，验证了多跨度识别任务和超双曲模型在泡沫检测中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: CryptoBubbles数据集和超双曲模型为社交媒体驱动的金融泡沫检测提供了新范式，可扩展至其他高波动性资产分析场景。

authors_text: Hans Weytjens,Jochen De Weerdt
title: Learning Uncertainty with Artificial Neural Networks for Improved Predictive Process Monitoring
title_zh: 使用人工神经网络学习不确定性以改进预测过程监控
abstract: The inability of artificial neural networks to assess the uncertainty of their predictions is an impediment to their widespread use. We distinguish two types of learnable uncertainty: model uncertainty due to a lack of training data and noise-induced observational uncertainty. Bayesian neural networks use solid mathematical foundations to learn the model uncertainties of their predictions. The observational uncertainty can be calculated by adding one layer to these networks and augmenting their loss functions. Our contribution is to apply these uncertainty concepts to predictive process monitoring tasks to train uncertainty-based models to predict the remaining time and outcomes. Our experiments show that uncertainty estimates allow more and less accurate predictions to be differentiated and confidence intervals to be constructed in both regression and classification tasks. These conclusions remain true even in early stages of running processes. Moreover, the deployed techniques are fast and produce more accurate predictions. The learned uncertainty could increase users' confidence in their process prediction systems, promote better cooperation between humans and these systems, and enable earlier implementations with smaller datasets.
summary: The inability of artificial neural networks to assess the uncertainty of their predictions is an impediment to their widespread use. We distinguish two types of learnable uncertainty: model uncertainty due to a lack of training data and noise-induced observational uncertainty. Bayesian neural networks use solid mathematical foundations to learn the model uncertainties of their predictions. The observational uncertainty can be calculated by adding one layer to these networks and augmenting their loss functions. Our contribution is to apply these uncertainty concepts to predictive process monitoring tasks to train uncertainty-based models to predict the remaining time and outcomes. Our experiments show that uncertainty estimates allow more and less accurate predictions to be differentiated and confidence intervals to be constructed in both regression and classification tasks. These conclusions remain true even in early stages of running processes. Moreover, the deployed techniques are fast and produce more accurate predictions. The learned uncertainty could increase users' confidence in their process prediction systems, promote better cooperation between humans and these systems, and enable earlier implementations with smaller datasets.
abstract_zh: 人工神经网络无法评估其预测的不确定性，这阻碍了其广泛使用。我们区分两种类型的可学习不确定性：由于缺乏训练数据而导致的模型不确定性和噪声引起的观测不确定性。贝叶斯神经网络使用坚实的数学基础来学习其预测的模型不确定性。观测不确定性可以通过向这些网络添加一层并增强其损失函数来计算。我们的贡献是将这些不确定性概念应用于预测过程监控任务，以训练基于不确定性的模型来预测剩余时间和结果。我们的实验表明，不确定性估计允许区分或多或少准确的预测，并在回归和分类任务中构建置信区间。即使在运行过程的早期阶段，这些结论仍然成立。此外，所部署的技术速度快并且可以产生更准确的预测。学到的不确定性可以增加用户对其过程预测系统的信心，促进人类与这些系统之间更好的合作，并能够使用较小的数据集进行早期实现。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合贝叶斯神经网络与增强损失函数的方法，用于预测过程监控中的不确定性建模，以提升预测的可靠性与准确性。
AI.motivation: 传统人工神经网络难以评估预测不确定性，导致其在过程监控等关键任务中应用受限，需解决模型不确定性与观测噪声的量化问题。
AI.method: 通过贝叶斯神经网络学习模型不确定性，添加网络层及改进损失函数以计算观测不确定性，并将该框架应用于预测过程监控的剩余时间与结果预测。
AI.result: 实验表明，所提方法可有效区分预测准确性、构建置信区间，且在过程早期阶段仍保持有效性，同时具备计算高效性与更高预测精度。
AI.conclusion: 不确定性建模显著提升过程预测系统的可信度与人机协作效率，尤其适用于小数据集场景下的早期预测实施。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种结合贝叶斯神经网络与增强损失函数的方法，用于预测过程监控中的不确定性建模，以提升预测的可靠性与准确性。

authors_text: Wayne Xin Zhao,Kun Zhou,Zheng Gong,Beichen Zhang,Yuanhang Zhou,Jing Sha,Zhigang Chen,Shijin Wang,Cong Liu,Ji-Rong Wen
title: JiuZhang: A Chinese Pre-trained Language Model for Mathematical Problem Understanding
title_zh: 九章：用于数学问题理解的中文预训练语言模型
abstract: This paper aims to advance the mathematical intelligence of machines by presenting the first Chinese mathematical pre-trained language model~(PLM) for effectively understanding and representing mathematical problems. Unlike other standard NLP tasks, mathematical texts are difficult to understand, since they involve mathematical terminology, symbols and formulas in the problem statement. Typically, it requires complex mathematical logic and background knowledge for solving mathematical problems. Considering the complex nature of mathematical texts, we design a novel curriculum pre-training approach for improving the learning of mathematical PLMs, consisting of both basic and advanced courses. Specially, we first perform token-level pre-training based on a position-biased masking strategy, and then design logic-based pre-training tasks that aim to recover the shuffled sentences and formulas, respectively. Finally, we introduce a more difficult pre-training task that enforces the PLM to detect and correct the errors in its generated solutions. We conduct extensive experiments on offline evaluation (including nine math-related tasks) and online $A/B$ test. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach compared with a number of competitive baselines. Our code is available at: \textcolor{blue}{\url{https://github.com/RUCAIBox/JiuZhang}}.
summary: This paper aims to advance the mathematical intelligence of machines by presenting the first Chinese mathematical pre-trained language model~(PLM) for effectively understanding and representing mathematical problems. Unlike other standard NLP tasks, mathematical texts are difficult to understand, since they involve mathematical terminology, symbols and formulas in the problem statement. Typically, it requires complex mathematical logic and background knowledge for solving mathematical problems. Considering the complex nature of mathematical texts, we design a novel curriculum pre-training approach for improving the learning of mathematical PLMs, consisting of both basic and advanced courses. Specially, we first perform token-level pre-training based on a position-biased masking strategy, and then design logic-based pre-training tasks that aim to recover the shuffled sentences and formulas, respectively. Finally, we introduce a more difficult pre-training task that enforces the PLM to detect and correct the errors in its generated solutions. We conduct extensive experiments on offline evaluation (including nine math-related tasks) and online $A/B$ test. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach compared with a number of competitive baselines. Our code is available at: \textcolor{blue}{\url{https://github.com/RUCAIBox/JiuZhang}}.
abstract_zh: 本文旨在通过提出第一个中国数学预训练语言模型（PLM）来提高机器的数学智能，以有效理解和表示数学问题。与其他标准 NLP 任务不同，数学文本很难理解，因为它们在问题陈述中涉及数学术语、符号和公式。通常，它需要复杂的数学逻辑和背景知识来解决数学问题。考虑到数学文本的复杂性，我们设计了一种新颖的课程预训练方法来提高数学PLM的学习，包括基础课程和高级课程。特别地，我们首先基于位置偏向掩码策略进行标记级预训练，然后设计基于逻辑的预训练任务，旨在分别恢复打乱的句子和公式。最后，我们引入一个更困难的预训练任务，强制 PLM 检测并纠正其生成的解决方案中的错误。我们对离线评估（包括九项数学相关任务）和在线 $A/B$ 测试进行了广泛的实验。实验结果证明了我们的方法与许多竞争基线相比的有效性。我们的代码位于：\textcolor{blue}{\url{https://github.com/RUCAIBox/JiuZhang}}。

AI.tldr: 本文提出首个中文数学预训练语言模型JiuZhang，旨在提升机器对数学问题的理解与表征能力。
AI.motivation: 数学文本因包含专业术语、符号和公式，且需复杂逻辑与背景知识，导致传统NLP模型难以有效处理数学问题。
AI.method: 设计分阶段课程预训练方法，包含基于位置偏置掩码的词元级预训练、逻辑恢复任务（恢复打乱句子与公式）及错误检测修正任务。
AI.result: 在九个数学相关任务和在线A/B测试中，JiuZhang相较多个基线模型表现出显著优势。
AI.conclusion: 课程预训练策略有效提升了模型对数学问题的理解能力，为中文数学智能发展提供新方法。

search_text: JiuZhang: A Chinese Pre-trained Language Model for Mathematical Problem Understanding 九章：用于数学问题理解的中文预训练语言模型 This paper aims to advance the mathematical intelligence of machines by presenting the first Chinese mathematical pre-trained language model~(PLM) for effectively understanding and representing mathematical problems. Unlike other standard NLP tasks, mathematical texts are difficult to understand, since they involve mathematical terminology, symbols and formulas in the problem statement. Typically, it requires complex mathematical logic and background knowledge for solving mathematical problems. Considering the complex nature of mathematical texts, we design a novel curriculum pre-training approach for improving the learning of mathematical PLMs, consisting of both basic and advanced courses. Specially, we first perform token-level pre-training based on a position-biased masking strategy, and then design logic-based pre-training tasks that aim to recover the shuffled sentences and formulas, respectively. Finally, we introduce a more difficult pre-training task that enforces the PLM to detect and correct the errors in its generated solutions. We conduct extensive experiments on offline evaluation (including nine math-related tasks) and online $A/B$ test. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach compared with a number of competitive baselines. Our code is available at: \textcolor{blue}{\url{https://github.com/RUCAIBox/JiuZhang}}. 本文旨在通过提出第一个中国数学预训练语言模型（PLM）来提高机器的数学智能，以有效理解和表示数学问题。与其他标准 NLP 任务不同，数学文本很难理解，因为它们在问题陈述中涉及数学术语、符号和公式。通常，它需要复杂的数学逻辑和背景知识来解决数学问题。考虑到数学文本的复杂性，我们设计了一种新颖的课程预训练方法来提高数学PLM的学习，包括基础课程和高级课程。特别地，我们首先基于位置偏向掩码策略进行标记级预训练，然后设计基于逻辑的预训练任务，旨在分别恢复打乱的句子和公式。最后，我们引入一个更困难的预训练任务，强制 PLM 检测并纠正其生成的解决方案中的错误。我们对离线评估（包括九项数学相关任务）和在线 $A/B$ 测试进行了广泛的实验。实验结果证明了我们的方法与许多竞争基线相比的有效性。我们的代码位于：\textcolor{blue}{\url{https://github.com/RUCAIBox/JiuZhang}}。 本文提出首个中文数学预训练语言模型JiuZhang，旨在提升机器对数学问题的理解与表征能力。 Wayne Xin Zhao,Kun Zhou,Zheng Gong,Beichen Zhang,Yuanhang Zhou,Jing Sha,Zhigang Chen,Shijin Wang,Cong Liu,Ji-Rong Wen cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Kaveri Kale,Pushpak Bhattacharyya,Aditya Shetty,Milind Gune,Kush Shrivastava,Rustom Lawyer,Spriha Biswas
title: Knowledge Graph Construction and Its Application in Automatic Radiology Report Generation from Radiologist's Dictation
title_zh: 知识图谱构建及其在放射科医生听写自动生成放射学报告中的应用
abstract: Conventionally, the radiologist prepares the diagnosis notes and shares them with the transcriptionist. Then the transcriptionist prepares a preliminary formatted report referring to the notes, and finally, the radiologist reviews the report, corrects the errors, and signs off. This workflow causes significant delays and errors in the report. In current research work, we focus on applications of NLP techniques like Information Extraction (IE) and domain-specific Knowledge Graph (KG) to automatically generate radiology reports from radiologist's dictation. This paper focuses on KG construction for each organ by extracting information from an existing large corpus of free-text radiology reports. We develop an information extraction pipeline that combines rule-based, pattern-based, and dictionary-based techniques with lexical-semantic features to extract entities and relations. Missing information in short dictation can be accessed from the KGs to generate pathological descriptions and hence the radiology report. Generated pathological descriptions evaluated using semantic similarity metrics, which shows 97% similarity with gold standard pathological descriptions. Also, our analysis shows that our IE module is performing better than the OpenIE tool for the radiology domain. Furthermore, we include a manual qualitative analysis from radiologists, which shows that 80-85% of the generated reports are correctly written, and the remaining are partially correct.
summary: Conventionally, the radiologist prepares the diagnosis notes and shares them with the transcriptionist. Then the transcriptionist prepares a preliminary formatted report referring to the notes, and finally, the radiologist reviews the report, corrects the errors, and signs off. This workflow causes significant delays and errors in the report. In current research work, we focus on applications of NLP techniques like Information Extraction (IE) and domain-specific Knowledge Graph (KG) to automatically generate radiology reports from radiologist's dictation. This paper focuses on KG construction for each organ by extracting information from an existing large corpus of free-text radiology reports. We develop an information extraction pipeline that combines rule-based, pattern-based, and dictionary-based techniques with lexical-semantic features to extract entities and relations. Missing information in short dictation can be accessed from the KGs to generate pathological descriptions and hence the radiology report. Generated pathological descriptions evaluated using semantic similarity metrics, which shows 97% similarity with gold standard pathological descriptions. Also, our analysis shows that our IE module is performing better than the OpenIE tool for the radiology domain. Furthermore, we include a manual qualitative analysis from radiologists, which shows that 80-85% of the generated reports are correctly written, and the remaining are partially correct.
abstract_zh: 按照惯例，放射科医生准备诊断记录并与转录员分享。然后，转录员参考笔记准备一份初步格式化的报告，最后，放射科医生审查该报告，更正错误并签字。此工作流程会导致报告出现严重延迟和错误。在当前的研究工作中，我们重点关注信息提取（IE）和特定领域知识图（KG）等自然语言处理技术的应用，以根据放射科医生的听写自动生成放射学报告。本文通过从现有的大型自由文本放射学报告语料库中提取信息，重点关注每个器官的知识图谱构建。我们开发了一种信息提取管道，它将基于规则、基于模式和基于字典的技术与词汇语义特征相结合来提取实体和关系。简短听写中缺失的信息可以从 KG 中获取，以生成病理描述，从而生成放射学报告。使用语义相似性度量评估生成的病理描​​述，其与金标准病理描述的相似度为 97%。此外，我们的分析表明，我们的 IE 模块在放射学领域的性能优于 OpenIE 工具。此外，我们还包括放射科医生的手动定性分析，结果表明生成的报告中有 80-85% 的书写正确，其余部分部分正确。

AI.tldr: 本论文提出利用自然语言处理技术（如信息抽取和领域知识图谱）从放射科医生的口述中自动生成放射学报告。
AI.motivation: 传统放射学报告生成流程依赖人工转录和校对，导致效率低下和错误频发，亟需自动化解决方案以提高准确性和效率。
AI.method: 构建针对每个器官的领域知识图谱，结合基于规则、模式和词典的信息抽取方法，提取实体与关系，并利用知识图谱补充短口述中的缺失信息生成病理描述。
AI.result: 生成的病理描述与标准描述的语义相似度达97%，信息抽取模块性能优于OpenIE工具，80-85%的生成报告经放射科医生评估为正确。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效提升了放射学报告生成的准确性与效率，知识图谱与信息抽取技术的结合在临床应用中具有显著潜力。

authors_text: Weifu Wang,Ping Li
title: Towards Constructing Finer then Homotopy Path Classes
title_zh: 构建更精细的同伦路径类
abstract: This work presents a new path classification criterion to distinguish paths geometrically and topologically from the workspace, which is divided through cell decomposition, generating a medial-axis-like skeleton structure. We use this information as well as the topology of the robot to bound and classify different paths in the configuration space. We show that the path class found by the proposed method is equivalent to and finer than the path class defined by the homotopy of paths. The proposed path classes are easy to compute, compare, and can be used for various planning purposes. The classification builds heavily upon the topology of the robot and the geometry of the workspace, leading to an alternative fiber-bundle-based description of the configuration space. We introduce a planning framework to overcome obstacles and narrow passages using the proposed path classification method and the resulting path classes.
summary: This work presents a new path classification criterion to distinguish paths geometrically and topologically from the workspace, which is divided through cell decomposition, generating a medial-axis-like skeleton structure. We use this information as well as the topology of the robot to bound and classify different paths in the configuration space. We show that the path class found by the proposed method is equivalent to and finer than the path class defined by the homotopy of paths. The proposed path classes are easy to compute, compare, and can be used for various planning purposes. The classification builds heavily upon the topology of the robot and the geometry of the workspace, leading to an alternative fiber-bundle-based description of the configuration space. We introduce a planning framework to overcome obstacles and narrow passages using the proposed path classification method and the resulting path classes.
abstract_zh: 这项工作提出了一种新的路径分类标准，从几何和拓扑上区分路径与工作空间，通过单元分解进行划分，生成类似中轴的骨架结构。我们使用这些信息以及机器人的拓扑来对配置空间中的不同路径进行绑定和分类。我们表明，所提出的方法找到的路径类相当于并且比路径同伦定义的路径类更精细。所提出的路径类别易于计算、比较，并且可用于各种规划目的。该分类很大程度上建立在机器人的拓扑结构和工作空间的几何形状的基础上，从而产生了另一种基于光纤束的配置空间描述。我们引入了一个规划框架，使用所提出的路径分类方法和生成的路径类别来克服障碍和狭窄通道。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种新的路径分类标准，通过几何和拓扑方法区分工作空间中的路径，生成类似中轴的骨架结构，并基于机器人拓扑和工作空间几何构建配置空间的纤维丛描述。
AI.motivation: 传统同伦路径分类无法精确区分复杂工作空间中的路径，需更细粒度的分类方法以提升路径规划效率，特别是在障碍物和狭窄通道场景中。
AI.method: 通过单元分解生成骨架结构，结合机器人拓扑和工作空间几何信息，构建配置空间的纤维丛描述，并设计基于该分类的路径规划框架。
AI.result: 提出的路径分类方法在保持同伦等价性的同时更精细，计算简便且适用于多种规划任务，为配置空间提供了新的数学描述。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过融合几何与拓扑信息，实现了更高效的路径分类与规划，为复杂环境下的机器人导航提供了新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种新的路径分类标准，通过几何和拓扑方法区分工作空间中的路径，生成类似中轴的骨架结构，并基于机器人拓扑和工作空间几何构建配置空间的纤维丛描述。
ai_content.motivation: 传统同伦路径分类无法精确区分复杂工作空间中的路径，需更细粒度的分类方法以提升路径规划效率，特别是在障碍物和狭窄通道场景中。
ai_content.method: 通过单元分解生成骨架结构，结合机器人拓扑和工作空间几何信息，构建配置空间的纤维丛描述，并设计基于该分类的路径规划框架。
ai_content.result: 提出的路径分类方法在保持同伦等价性的同时更精细，计算简便且适用于多种规划任务，为配置空间提供了新的数学描述。
ai_content.conclusion: 该方法通过融合几何与拓扑信息，实现了更高效的路径分类与规划，为复杂环境下的机器人导航提供了新思路。

search_text: Towards Constructing Finer then Homotopy Path Classes 构建更精细的同伦路径类 This work presents a new path classification criterion to distinguish paths geometrically and topologically from the workspace, which is divided through cell decomposition, generating a medial-axis-like skeleton structure. We use this information as well as the topology of the robot to bound and classify different paths in the configuration space. We show that the path class found by the proposed method is equivalent to and finer than the path class defined by the homotopy of paths. The proposed path classes are easy to compute, compare, and can be used for various planning purposes. The classification builds heavily upon the topology of the robot and the geometry of the workspace, leading to an alternative fiber-bundle-based description of the configuration space. We introduce a planning framework to overcome obstacles and narrow passages using the proposed path classification method and the resulting path classes. 这项工作提出了一种新的路径分类标准，从几何和拓扑上区分路径与工作空间，通过单元分解进行划分，生成类似中轴的骨架结构。我们使用这些信息以及机器人的拓扑来对配置空间中的不同路径进行绑定和分类。我们表明，所提出的方法找到的路径类相当于并且比路径同伦定义的路径类更精细。所提出的路径类别易于计算、比较，并且可用于各种规划目的。该分类很大程度上建立在机器人的拓扑结构和工作空间的几何形状的基础上，从而产生了另一种基于光纤束的配置空间描述。我们引入了一个规划框架，使用所提出的路径分类方法和生成的路径类别来克服障碍和狭窄通道。 本文提出一种新的路径分类标准，通过几何和拓扑方法区分工作空间中的路径，生成类似中轴的骨架结构，并基于机器人拓扑和工作空间几何构建配置空间的纤维丛描述。 Weifu Wang,Ping Li cs.RO

authors_text: Aida Manzano Kharman,Christian Jursitzky,Quan Zhou,Pietro Ferraro,Jakub Marecek,Pierre Pinson,Robert Shorten
title: An adversarially robust data-market for spatial, crowd-sourced data
title_zh: 空间众包数据的对抗性强大数据市场
abstract: We describe an architecture for a decentralised data market for applications in which agents are incentivised to collaborate to crowd-source their data. The architecture is designed to reward data that furthers the market's collective goal, and distributes reward fairly to all those that contribute with their data. We show that the architecture is resilient to Sybil, wormhole, and data poisoning attacks. In order to evaluate the resilience of the architecture, we characterise its breakdown points for various adversarial threat models in an automotive use case.
summary: We describe an architecture for a decentralised data market for applications in which agents are incentivised to collaborate to crowd-source their data. The architecture is designed to reward data that furthers the market's collective goal, and distributes reward fairly to all those that contribute with their data. We show that the architecture is resilient to Sybil, wormhole, and data poisoning attacks. In order to evaluate the resilience of the architecture, we characterise its breakdown points for various adversarial threat models in an automotive use case.
abstract_zh: 我们描述了一种应用程序的去中心化数据市场的架构，在该架构中，代理被激励协作以众包其数据。该架构旨在奖励促进市场集体目标的数据，并将奖励公平地分配给所有贡献数据的人。我们证明该架构能够抵御 Sybil、虫洞和数据中毒攻击。为了评估该架构的弹性，我们描述了汽车用例中各种对抗威胁模型的故障点。

AI.tldr: 本文设计了一个去中心化的数据市场架构，针对空间众包数据场景，通过激励机制和攻击防御策略提升系统的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有数据市场面临对抗攻击威胁（如Sybil、数据投毒），且难以公平激励参与者贡献高质量数据，需构建安全且激励相容的众包数据系统。
AI.method: 提出结合博弈论激励机制与对抗鲁棒性设计的架构，通过动态奖励分配和攻击检测模块，利用汽车场景下的对抗威胁模型验证系统有效性。
AI.result: 架构成功抵御Sybil、wormhole和数据投毒攻击，在汽车用例中验证了其在对抗攻击下的稳定性与数据质量保障能力。
AI.conclusion: 该架构为安全众包数据市场提供了新范式，通过理论分析与实证验证，证明其在对抗环境下的可行性与有效性。

authors_text: Kyurae Kim,Jisu Oh,Jacob R. Gardner,Adji Bousso Dieng,Hongseok Kim
title: Markov Chain Score Ascent: A Unifying Framework of Variational Inference with Markovian Gradients
title_zh: 马尔可夫链得分上升：马尔可夫梯度变分推理的统一框架
abstract: Minimizing the inclusive Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence with stochastic gradient descent (SGD) is challenging since its gradient is defined as an integral over the posterior. Recently, multiple methods have been proposed to run SGD with biased gradient estimates obtained from a Markov chain. This paper provides the first non-asymptotic convergence analysis of these methods by establishing their mixing rate and gradient variance. To do this, we demonstrate that these methods-which we collectively refer to as Markov chain score ascent (MCSA) methods-can be cast as special cases of the Markov chain gradient descent framework. Furthermore, by leveraging this new understanding, we develop a novel MCSA scheme, parallel MCSA (pMCSA), that achieves a tighter bound on the gradient variance. We demonstrate that this improved theoretical result translates to superior empirical performance.
summary: Minimizing the inclusive Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence with stochastic gradient descent (SGD) is challenging since its gradient is defined as an integral over the posterior. Recently, multiple methods have been proposed to run SGD with biased gradient estimates obtained from a Markov chain. This paper provides the first non-asymptotic convergence analysis of these methods by establishing their mixing rate and gradient variance. To do this, we demonstrate that these methods-which we collectively refer to as Markov chain score ascent (MCSA) methods-can be cast as special cases of the Markov chain gradient descent framework. Furthermore, by leveraging this new understanding, we develop a novel MCSA scheme, parallel MCSA (pMCSA), that achieves a tighter bound on the gradient variance. We demonstrate that this improved theoretical result translates to superior empirical performance.
abstract_zh: 使用随机梯度下降 (SGD) 最小化包容性 Kullback-Leibler (KL) 散度具有挑战性，因为其梯度被定义为后验积分。最近，人们提出了多种方法来使用从马尔可夫链获得的有偏差梯度估计来运行 SGD。本文通过建立这些方法的混合率和梯度方差，首次对这些方法进行了非渐近收敛分析。为此，我们证明这些方法（我们统称为马尔可夫链得分上升（MCSA）方法）可以作为马尔可夫链梯度下降框架的特例。此外，通过利用这种新的理解，我们开发了一种新颖的 MCSA 方案，即并行 MCSA (pMCSA)，它实现了对梯度方差的更严格限制。我们证明，这种改进的理论结果可以转化为卓越的经验表现。

AI.tldr: 提出马尔可夫链得分上升（MCSA）框架，统一分析变分推断中的马尔可夫梯度方法，并改进其收敛性分析。
AI.motivation: 传统变分推断中使用随机梯度下降优化KL散度时面临梯度估计困难，现有基于马尔可夫链的有偏梯度估计方法缺乏非渐近收敛性理论分析。
AI.method: 将MCSA方法归类为马尔可夫链梯度下降框架，分析混合速率与梯度方差，提出并行MCSA（pMCSA）降低方差上界。
AI.result: 理论证明pMCSA具有更紧的梯度方差界，实验验证其优于现有方法的收敛性能。
AI.conclusion: 该框架为马尔可夫梯度方法提供统一理论分析，pMCSA通过并行化实现更优的变分推断效果。
