authors_text: Ikki Kishida,Hideki Nakayama
title: Pixel to Binary Embedding Towards Robustness for CNNs
title_zh: 像素到二进制嵌入以提高 CNN 的鲁棒性
abstract: There are several problems with the robustness of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). For example, the prediction of CNNs can be changed by adding a small magnitude of noise to an input, and the performances of CNNs are degraded when the distribution of input is shifted by a transformation never seen during training (e.g., the blur effect). There are approaches to replace pixel values with binary embeddings to tackle the problem of adversarial perturbations, which successfully improve robustness. In this work, we propose Pixel to Binary Embedding (P2BE) to improve the robustness of CNNs. P2BE is a learnable binary embedding method as opposed to previous hand-coded binary embedding methods. P2BE outperforms other binary embedding methods in robustness against adversarial perturbations and visual corruptions that are not shown during training.
summary: There are several problems with the robustness of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). For example, the prediction of CNNs can be changed by adding a small magnitude of noise to an input, and the performances of CNNs are degraded when the distribution of input is shifted by a transformation never seen during training (e.g., the blur effect). There are approaches to replace pixel values with binary embeddings to tackle the problem of adversarial perturbations, which successfully improve robustness. In this work, we propose Pixel to Binary Embedding (P2BE) to improve the robustness of CNNs. P2BE is a learnable binary embedding method as opposed to previous hand-coded binary embedding methods. P2BE outperforms other binary embedding methods in robustness against adversarial perturbations and visual corruptions that are not shown during training.
abstract_zh: 卷积神经网络（CNN）的鲁棒性存在几个问题。例如，可以通过向输入添加少量噪声来改变 CNN 的预测，并且当输入的分布因训练期间从未见过的变换（例如模糊效果）而发生变化时，CNN 的性能会下降。有一些方法可以用二进制嵌入替换像素值来解决对抗性扰动问题，从而成功地提高了鲁棒性。在这项工作中，我们提出像素到二进制嵌入（P2BE）来提高 CNN 的鲁棒性。 P2BE 是一种可学习的二进制嵌入方法，而不是以前的手工编码的二进制嵌入方法。 P2BE 在对抗训练期间未显示的对抗性扰动和视觉损坏的鲁棒性方面优于其他二进制嵌入方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种可学习的二进制嵌入方法P2BE，用于提升卷积神经网络（CNN）在对抗扰动和视觉损坏下的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: CNN在面对对抗样本或输入分布变化时表现脆弱，现有二进制嵌入方法多为手工设计，难以有效应对复杂扰动。
AI.method: 设计可学习的Pixel to Binary Embedding（P2BE）方法，将像素值转换为二进制嵌入表示，替代传统手工编码方案。
AI.result: P2BE在对抗扰动和未见过的视觉损坏场景中，显著优于其他二进制嵌入方法，有效提升CNN鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: P2BE通过可学习的二进制嵌入机制，成功增强了CNN对输入扰动和分布偏移的鲁棒性，优于现有方法。

search_text: Pixel to Binary Embedding Towards Robustness for CNNs 像素到二进制嵌入以提高 CNN 的鲁棒性 There are several problems with the robustness of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). For example, the prediction of CNNs can be changed by adding a small magnitude of noise to an input, and the performances of CNNs are degraded when the distribution of input is shifted by a transformation never seen during training (e.g., the blur effect). There are approaches to replace pixel values with binary embeddings to tackle the problem of adversarial perturbations, which successfully improve robustness. In this work, we propose Pixel to Binary Embedding (P2BE) to improve the robustness of CNNs. P2BE is a learnable binary embedding method as opposed to previous hand-coded binary embedding methods. P2BE outperforms other binary embedding methods in robustness against adversarial perturbations and visual corruptions that are not shown during training. 卷积神经网络（CNN）的鲁棒性存在几个问题。例如，可以通过向输入添加少量噪声来改变 CNN 的预测，并且当输入的分布因训练期间从未见过的变换（例如模糊效果）而发生变化时，CNN 的性能会下降。有一些方法可以用二进制嵌入替换像素值来解决对抗性扰动问题，从而成功地提高了鲁棒性。在这项工作中，我们提出像素到二进制嵌入（P2BE）来提高 CNN 的鲁棒性。 P2BE 是一种可学习的二进制嵌入方法，而不是以前的手工编码的二进制嵌入方法。 P2BE 在对抗训练期间未显示的对抗性扰动和视觉损坏的鲁棒性方面优于其他二进制嵌入方法。 本文提出一种可学习的二进制嵌入方法P2BE，用于提升卷积神经网络（CNN）在对抗扰动和视觉损坏下的鲁棒性。 Ikki Kishida,Hideki Nakayama cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Lin Tian,Hastings Greer,François-Xavier Vialard,Roland Kwitt,Raúl San José Estépar,Richard Jarrett Rushmore,Nikolaos Makris,Sylvain Bouix,Marc Niethammer
title: $\texttt{GradICON}$: Approximate Diffeomorphisms via Gradient Inverse Consistency
title_zh: $\texttt{GradICON}$：通过梯度逆一致性近似微分同胚
abstract: We present an approach to learning regular spatial transformations between image pairs in the context of medical image registration. Contrary to optimization-based registration techniques and many modern learning-based methods, we do not directly penalize transformation irregularities but instead promote transformation regularity via an inverse consistency penalty. We use a neural network to predict a map between a source and a target image as well as the map when swapping the source and target images. Different from existing approaches, we compose these two resulting maps and regularize deviations of the $\bf{Jacobian}$ of this composition from the identity matrix. This regularizer -- $\texttt{GradICON}$ -- results in much better convergence when training registration models compared to promoting inverse consistency of the composition of maps directly while retaining the desirable implicit regularization effects of the latter. We achieve state-of-the-art registration performance on a variety of real-world medical image datasets using a single set of hyperparameters and a single non-dataset-specific training protocol.
summary: We present an approach to learning regular spatial transformations between image pairs in the context of medical image registration. Contrary to optimization-based registration techniques and many modern learning-based methods, we do not directly penalize transformation irregularities but instead promote transformation regularity via an inverse consistency penalty. We use a neural network to predict a map between a source and a target image as well as the map when swapping the source and target images. Different from existing approaches, we compose these two resulting maps and regularize deviations of the $\bf{Jacobian}$ of this composition from the identity matrix. This regularizer -- $\texttt{GradICON}$ -- results in much better convergence when training registration models compared to promoting inverse consistency of the composition of maps directly while retaining the desirable implicit regularization effects of the latter. We achieve state-of-the-art registration performance on a variety of real-world medical image datasets using a single set of hyperparameters and a single non-dataset-specific training protocol.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种在医学图像配准的背景下学习图像对之间的规则空间变换的方法。与基于优化的配准技术和许多基于学习的现代方法相反，我们不直接惩罚变换不规则性，而是通过逆一致性惩罚来促进变换规律性。我们使用神经网络来预测源图像和目标图像之间的映射以及交换源图像和目标图像时的映射。与现有方法不同，我们组合这两个结果图，并规范化该组合的 $\bf{Jacobian}$ 与单位矩阵的偏差。与直接促进映射组成的逆一致性同时保留后者所需的隐式正则化效果相比，该正则化器 - $\texttt{GradICON}$ 在训练配准模型时会产生更好的收敛性。我们使用一组超参数和一个非数据集特定的训练协议在各种现实世界的医学图像数据集上实现了最先进的配准性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为GradICON的新方法，通过梯度逆一致性正则化学习医学图像配准中的正则空间变换。
AI.motivation: 传统医学图像配准方法在处理变换不规则性时存在收敛困难或正则化效果不足的问题，需寻找更有效的变换正则化策略。
AI.method: 使用神经网络预测源图像与目标图像之间的双向映射，通过组合双向映射并正则化其雅可比矩阵与单位矩阵的偏差，实现梯度逆一致性约束。
AI.result: 在多个真实医学图像数据集上达到最先进配准效果，且仅需单一超参数集和训练协议即可适应不同数据集。
AI.conclusion: GradICON通过雅可比矩阵正则化有效提升了配准模型收敛性，同时保持了隐式正则化优势，在医学图像配准任务中表现优异。

authors_text: Jiawei Liu,Kaiyu Zhang,Weitai Hu,Qing Yang
title: Improve Ranking Correlation of Super-net through Training Scheme from One-shot NAS to Few-shot NAS
title_zh: 通过从One-shot NAS到Few-shot NAS的训练方案提高超网的排名相关性
abstract: The algorithms of one-shot neural architecture search(NAS) have been widely used to reduce computation consumption. However, because of the interference among the subnets in which weights are shared, the subnets inherited from these super-net trained by those algorithms have poor consistency in precision ranking. To address this problem, we propose a step-by-step training super-net scheme from one-shot NAS to few-shot NAS. In the training scheme, we firstly train super-net in a one-shot way, and then we disentangle the weights of super-net by splitting them into multi-subnets and training them gradually. Finally, our method ranks 4th place in the CVPR2022 3rd Lightweight NAS Challenge Track1. Our code is available at https://github.com/liujiawei2333/CVPR2022-NAS-competition-Track-1-4th-solution.
summary: The algorithms of one-shot neural architecture search(NAS) have been widely used to reduce computation consumption. However, because of the interference among the subnets in which weights are shared, the subnets inherited from these super-net trained by those algorithms have poor consistency in precision ranking. To address this problem, we propose a step-by-step training super-net scheme from one-shot NAS to few-shot NAS. In the training scheme, we firstly train super-net in a one-shot way, and then we disentangle the weights of super-net by splitting them into multi-subnets and training them gradually. Finally, our method ranks 4th place in the CVPR2022 3rd Lightweight NAS Challenge Track1. Our code is available at https://github.com/liujiawei2333/CVPR2022-NAS-competition-Track-1-4th-solution.
abstract_zh: 一次性神经架构搜索（NAS）算法已被广泛用于减少计算消耗。然而，由于共享权重的子网之间存在干扰，这些算法训练的这些超网继承的子网在精度排序方面一致性较差。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种从one-shot NAS到few-shot NAS的逐步训练超级网络方案。在训练方案中，我们首先以一次性方式训练超级网，然后通过将超级网分成多个子网并逐步训练来解开超级网的权重。最后，我们的方法在 CVPR2022 第三届轻量级 NAS Challenge Track1 中排名第四。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/liujiawei2333/CVPR2022-NAS-competition-Track-1-4th-solution 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种从一次性NAS到少次NAS的逐步训练超级网络方案，以提高子网络的精度排名一致性。
AI.motivation: 一次性NAS算法因子网络间权重共享导致精度排名不一致，影响模型性能。
AI.method: 首先以一次性方式训练超级网络，随后通过拆分权重为多子网络并逐步训练，实现权重解耦。
AI.result: 该方法在CVPR2022轻量级NAS挑战赛Track1中排名第四。
AI.conclusion: 逐步训练方案有效提升了子网络排名一致性，验证了方法在实际竞赛中的竞争力。
